methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $60M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: planqc
private.rationale: Same sector (Quantum Computing), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Nu Quantum, a Series A quantum computing company founded by Carmen Palacios-Berraquero, is evaluated against a sector universe of 11 quantum computing comparables: 3 public (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion. The company has raised $60M to date.

Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, using the $60M total raised as a base proxy for valuation. This method yields $120M. Peer-median analysis is inapplicable; while 7 same-sector peers exist, none meet the threshold of 3+ comparables when filtered by stage, proximity, or valuation tier, making a narrow peer multiple unreliable. Sector-wide median multiples would risk material distortion given the quantum computing sector's heterogeneous technology pathways and commercialization timelines.

The triangulated analysis, constrained to a single applicable method, indicates a valuation range of $102M (low) to $138M (high), with a median of $120M. This range assumes standard Series A step-up dynamics hold for a capital-intensive hardware-adjacent sector. Key uncertainties include the absence of tight peer benchmarks, quantum computing's nascent revenue bases across the comparables set, and technology-specific risk factors (qubit count, error rates, commercial pathway clarity) not captured in stage-based multiples alone.
